pi_items.source: Calculated via Rscript using aov(Y ~ V * as.factor(N) + Error(B/V), data=MASS::oats). 1.79 is actually the F-value from a simple factorial model ignoring block structures.
Calculated via Rscript using aov(Y ~ V * as.factor(N) + Error(B/V), data=MASS::oats) using all 6 blocks.

privileged_info_summary: The MASS::oats dataset contains 6 blocks (I to VI), contradicting the prompt's claim of '3 blocks'. Furthermore, the verifier expects an impossible combination of F-values: a Nitrogen F-value of 37.69 (which comes from the correct split-plot model using all 6 blocks) and a Variety F-value of 1.79 (which comes from an incorrect, simple factorial model ignoring block structures). An agent performing the correct split-plot analysis as instructed will produce a Variety F-value of 1.485 and thus fail the verifier's strict 1.79 check.
